title: anagyris foetida subsp. neapolitana
intro: Anagyris foetida subsp. neapolitana is a sub-species of the Anagyris foetida plant, also known as the stinking anise or stinking gum plant, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Anagyris foetida subsp. neapolitana include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress levels, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
